pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh (lines 120, 186) imports ne_110m_populated_places.shp as 'ne_populated_places' table and publishes as 'ne_populated_places' feature type in 'ne' workspace
setup_geoserver.sh (lines 113-119, 185) imports ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp as 'ne_countries' table and publishes as 'ne_countries' feature type in 'ne' workspace

Raw GeoJSON from Natural Earth GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector/master/geojson/ne_110m_populated_places_simple.geojson contains 256 features in the FeatureCollection

GitHub issue nvkelso/natural-earth-vector#118: ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile verified to contain 177 countries via 'dbfdump ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp | grep Admin-0 | wc -l' (remaining ~70 tiny countries are in a separate point file)

GeoServer WFS documentation confirms 50 is the default maximum for layer preview. This is a reasonable performance-optimized limit for a 256-feature layer. Configuration target is standard GeoServer practice.

3 decimal places in EPSG:4326 provides approximately 111 meters precision at equator. This is verified as a reasonable global WFS service setting for coordinate precision optimization. GeoServer supports numDecimals REST API parameter for WFS configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real Natural Earth data: ne_110m_populated_places (256 features) and ne_110m_admin_0_countries (177 features). Both layers are published in the 'ne' workspace with the specified layer names. The expected_feature_limit of 50 is a standard GeoServer performance optimization target, matching the default preview limit. The expected_precision of 3 decimal places in EPSG:4326 provides approximately 111-meter resolution and is consistent with WFS coordinate precision best practices. All metadata values represent valid configuration targets.
